author: y4rr
content:
Surfaced by C8.1 conformance test lifecycle::memory_guard_hook_fires_before_budget_error.

Root cause: MemoryGuard::run_hooks() decrements used_bytes via fetch_update(|c| Some(c.saturating_sub(freed))). When used_bytes is 0 (no prior safe_alloc tracked usage), saturating_sub keeps it at 0. The subsequent check_budget() call still reads used_bytes=0 and computes 0+alloc_bytes > budget, which is the same check that originally failed.

Result: safe_alloc_with_hooks always returns BudgetExceeded when the guard has no prior tracked allocations, regardless of how much a hook claims to free. The hook mechanism only works when there is pre-existing tracked usage for the hook to offset against.

Repro path in conformance test: budget=512, used_bytes=0, alloc=800 bytes, hook frees=1024 bytes -> still BudgetExceeded.

Fix direction: run_hooks should increase available headroom independently from used_bytes (e.g. track 'externally_freed_bytes' accumulator), or check_budget after hooks should compute: used_bytes + alloc_bytes - freed <= budget.
